problem_statement: AI-assisted development tools (vibe coding) generate code from natural language without verification; LLMs are stochastic generators with zero internal semantic verification capability, so errors go undetected until production — the 'verification gap'.
magnitude_evidence: 10.3% — 170 of 1,645 Lovable projects exposed sensitive data via misconfigured Row Level Security, totaling 303 vulnerable endpoints [Palmer, 2025]; METR RCT found a 19% slowdown despite developers perceiving a 20% speedup
insufficiency_evidence: experienced developers who used frontier AI models were measurably slower in objective evaluations despite believing they were faster [METR, 2025]; developers ask the AI if the design is good and the AI, through RLHF alignment, tends to agree (validation theater); relying exclusively on automated tests misses semantic adequacy and domain correctness
solution_mechanisms: external verification agents (VA) at discrete gates
hierarchical semantic analysis for problem discovery
cost-tier model switching after gate G4
notes: Conceptual/methodology paper with single-institution non-public validation (N=1 author calibrations); limitations formalized as untested hypotheses. Some garbled math notation in text.
